methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Dust is a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Gabriel Hubert and Stanislas Polu, having raised $70M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on two primary methods triangulated against a comparable universe of five same-sector, same-stage private peers (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, Photoroom) alongside one public reference point (PLTR).

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $70M total raised proxy, yielding $133M. Peer-median analysis across the five identified comparables produces a $5.0B implied valuation, with the peer range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within frontier AI: Safe Superintelligence commands $32.0B while Photoroom trades at $500M, suggesting meaningful differentiation by technical approach and market positioning.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $113M (floor, stage-progression anchored) to $5.8B (ceiling). The median implied value of $2.6B reflects meaningful tension between the conservative build-up from deployed capital and the elevated multiples observed in the peer set. Material uncertainties include Dust's revenue traction relative to stage peers, product-market fit substantiation, and the sustainability of current AI sector multiples. The peer-median approach carries execution risk given the $32B outlier (SSI) and whether Dust's positioning commands median or sub-median positioning within this volatile cohort.
